body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, for your awareness — incident report {CASE} was filed on {DATE} at {TIME} for retail theft at {BIZ}, {ADDRESS}. Loss prevention detained a {AGE}-year-old subject concealing merchandise valued at {AMOUNT}; recovered property was returned to the store. IUCR 0860 (retail theft under $300), reportable to ISP under NIBRS as larceny/shoplifting. Disposition: {DISPOSITION}. No injuries. Records will include this in the daily blotter and the monthly NIBRS roll-up. Let me know if you need the squad video pulled before any council mention.
reply_tmpl: Thanks for the heads up. No action needed from my office — just keep it in the monthly summary.

body_tmpl: Mayor, flagging incident {CASE} for your awareness. On {DATE} at approximately {TIME}, a resident at {ADDRESS} reported a forced-entry burglary — rear door pried open, electronics and cash taken, estimated loss {AMOUNT}. Classified IUCR 0610 (burglary/forcible entry), NIBRS 220. Detectives were notified, the scene was processed for latents, and a neighborhood canvass was conducted on {STREET}. Disposition: {DISPOSITION}. This is the second residential burglary in that area this month; I've asked the beat officer to add directed patrol. Happy to brief before the board meeting if useful.
reply_tmpl: Please do brief me — I'd like a one-line status before Tuesday. Good call on the directed patrol.

body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, incident report {CASE} documents an overnight commercial burglary at {BIZ}, {ADDRESS}, discovered {DATE} at {TIME}. The front glass door was shattered and the register drawer removed; estimated loss is {AMOUNT}. IUCR 0620 (burglary, unlawful entry), reported to ISP under NIBRS. Security footage from the {STREET} corridor was secured for review and detectives are following up with the owner. Disposition: {DISPOSITION}. No injuries reported. I'll keep this in the weekly index-crime detail for your review.

Mayor, for your awareness — report {CASE} was filed on {DATE} at {TIME} for a stolen motor vehicle taken from the 1200 block of {STREET}. Vehicle is a late-model SUV valued at approximately {AMOUNT}; owner reports the fob was not left inside. IUCR 0910 (motor-vehicle theft), NIBRS 240. Plate entered into LEADS as stolen and a regional BOLO was broadcast. Disposition: {DISPOSITION}. This is the third MVT in the village in two weeks — I'll have a short pattern memo for you by Friday. No suspect information yet.
reply_tmpl: Three in two weeks is worth a council heads-up. Send me the pattern memo and copy the village manager.

body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, incident {CASE} documents a catalytic-converter theft reported {DATE} at {TIME} from a vehicle parked in the driveway at {ADDRESS}. The converter was cut overnight; estimated repair cost is {AMOUNT}. Classified IUCR 0915 (theft of motor-vehicle parts), reported under NIBRS as larceny. No witnesses; area camera footage near {CROSS} was canvassed. Disposition: {DISPOSITION}. This makes five converter thefts in the village over the past week — I'm coordinating with the county auto-theft task force and will fold the totals into the weekly trend report.
reply_tmpl: Five in a week — let's get a community alert out so residents know to park in garages. Loop in the PIO.

subject_tmpl: Offense report {CASE} — criminal damage to vehicles, {STREET} corridor
body_tmpl: Mayor, for your awareness — report {CASE} covers criminal damage to property reported {DATE} at {TIME} along the {STREET} corridor near {CROSS}. A complainant reported a vehicle keyed with extensive paint damage to the driver-side panels, estimated at {AMOUNT}; two adjacent vehicles had mirrors broken off. IUCR 1320 (criminal damage to property), NIBRS 290. No witnesses identified and no suspects at this time. Disposition: {DISPOSITION}. Records is grouping these into the overnight criminal-damage detail; I'll note it in the weekly review.

Mayor Okonkwo, flagging incident {CASE} filed {DATE} at {TIME} for criminal damage to property at {ADDRESS}. A property owner reported graffiti spray-painted across the exterior wall facing {STREET}; photos were collected and estimated cleanup is {AMOUNT}. IUCR 1330 (criminal trespass to property / defacement), reported under NIBRS as vandalism. No suspects identified; the tag was photographed and submitted for comparison with prior incidents. Disposition: {DISPOSITION}. Public Works has been notified for abatement coordination. Keeping you in the loop given it's a visible block.
reply_tmpl: Thanks. Make sure PW gets it cleaned within 48 hours — let's not let it linger.

subject_tmpl: Heads up: battery report {CASE} filed near {CROSS}
body_tmpl: Mayor, for your awareness — incident {CASE} was filed {DATE} at {TIME} for a simple battery near {CROSS}. The victim, a {AGE}-year-old resident, reported being struck by a known individual following a verbal dispute; minor injuries, no transport required. Classified IUCR 0486 (domestic battery) / 0560 (simple battery) as appropriate, reported under NIBRS 13B. Officers separated the parties and an offender was identified. Disposition: {DISPOSITION}. No weapons involved. I'll keep this in the daily person-crime log; let me know if you want details before tonight's meeting.

subject_tmpl: Sensitive: aggravated battery report {CASE} — please brief before board
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, I want you briefed before tonight's board meeting on incident {CASE}, an aggravated battery filed {DATE} at {TIME} near {ADDRESS}. A {AGE}-year-old victim sustained serious injuries and was transported to Loyola; the incident appears to have stemmed from an ongoing dispute between known parties — not a random attack. IUCR 0410 (aggravated battery), NIBRS 13A. Detectives are assigned, an offender has been identified, and we expect charges. Disposition: {DISPOSITION}. Please treat as sensitive — I'd recommend a no-comment-beyond-the-facts posture if asked publicly. I can call you before the meeting.
reply_tmpl: Let's talk before the meeting — call my cell at 4:30. I'll defer all press to you and the attorney.

body_tmpl: Mayor, sensitive heads-up on incident {CASE}. On {DATE} at {TIME} officers responded to a 911 call for a domestic battery in progress at {ADDRESS}. The victim, age {AGE}, had visible injuries; the offender was placed under arrest for domestic battery and transported to Cook County. Order-of-protection information and DV resource referrals were provided on scene. IUCR 0486, reported under NIBRS and included in our ISP-mandated monthly domestic-crime data. Disposition: {DISPOSITION}. Please keep address details out of any public remarks given victim safety. No further action needed from your office.
reply_tmpl: Understood — I'll say nothing publicly. Thank the responding officers for handling it carefully.

body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, for your awareness — arrest report {CASE} was filed {DATE} at {TIME} for driving under the influence. An officer stopped a vehicle on {STREET} near {CROSS} after observing it weaving; the {AGE}-year-old driver failed field sobriety testing and registered above the legal limit on the breathalyzer. Statutory summary suspension paperwork was completed and the vehicle was towed. IUCR 2410 / 625 ILCS 5/11-501, reported per NIBRS. Disposition: {DISPOSITION}. This is part of our weekend enforcement totals; I'll include it in the monthly DUI summary.

Mayor, flagging incident {CASE} filed {DATE} at {TIME}. During a traffic stop on {STREET} for an equipment violation, an officer detected the odor of cannabis; a consent search yielded packaged controlled substances and a {AMOUNT} quantity of suspected narcotics. The {AGE}-year-old driver was arrested and transported for processing. IUCR 2020 (possession/delivery of controlled substance), reported under NIBRS. Evidence was inventoried with chain-of-custody and submitted to the ISP crime lab. Disposition: {DISPOSITION}. This was a routine stop, not a targeted detail. Keeping you informed for the council briefing.
reply_tmpl: Good work by the officer. Keep it in the briefing packet but no need to single it out publicly.

subject_tmpl: Offense report {CASE} — identity theft / fraud, constituent {LAST}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, for your awareness — report {CASE} documents a financial fraud / identity-theft complaint filed {DATE} at {TIME} by a resident, {NAME}, of {ADDRESS}. The complainant reported unauthorized accounts opened in their name and an attempted wire transfer totaling {AMOUNT}; the matter has been referred to the financial institution and to the Illinois Attorney General's identity-theft unit. IUCR 1130 (financial identity theft), reported under NIBRS as fraud. Disposition: {DISPOSITION}. We're seeing an uptick in these this month and I'll include the totals in the weekly fraud detail. The resident was given a victim packet and FTC referral.
reply_tmpl: Thanks. Given the uptick, let's get a senior-scam advisory drafted for the newsletter.

subject_tmpl: Incident report {CASE} — online scam / wire fraud, senior resident
body_tmpl: Mayor, flagging incident {CASE} filed {DATE} at {TIME}. An {AGE}-year-old resident at {ADDRESS} reported an online scam in which a caller impersonating a utility company induced a {AMOUNT} prepaid-card payment. Classified IUCR 1150 (deceptive practice), reported under NIBRS as fraud. The report was forwarded to the financial-crimes detective and the resident's bank was contacted to attempt a stop-payment. Disposition: {DISPOSITION}. This matches a pattern targeting seniors village-wide. I'd recommend a community-policing bulletin; I can have records draft one for your review.
reply_tmpl: Yes — draft the bulletin and route it through my office before it goes out.

body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, for your awareness — report {CASE} was filed {DATE} at {TIME} for disorderly conduct at {BIZ}, {ADDRESS}. A {AGE}-year-old patron created a disturbance and refused to leave after repeated requests; the subject was cited and escorted from the premises without further incident. IUCR 2890 (disorderly conduct), reported under NIBRS as a quality-of-life offense. No injuries, no property damage. Disposition: {DISPOSITION}. This is a recurring location for weekend nuisance calls; I'll fold it into the weekend disorderly recap.

subject_tmpl: URGENT: weapons offense report {CASE} — shots fired, {STREET}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, urgent awareness on incident {CASE}. At {TIME} on {DATE}, officers responded to a shots-fired call in the 200 block of {STREET}. Officers located spent shell casings; there is no victim and no property damage reported at this time. Evidence was packaged and submitted, and the investigation is open. Separately, a {AGE}-year-old subject was later taken into custody nearby in possession of an unlicensed handgun — IUCR 1430 / 1310 (UUW / reckless discharge), NIBRS weapons-law violation. Disposition: {DISPOSITION}. No injuries. I wanted you to have the facts before any social-media chatter reaches your office. I'll update as the investigation develops.
reply_tmpl: Appreciate the fast notice. Keep me posted hourly until you confirm no injuries publicly. Defer press to you.

subject_tmpl: Weapons report {CASE} — illegal firearm recovered, traffic stop on {STREET}
body_tmpl: Mayor, flagging incident {CASE} filed {DATE} at {TIME}. During a traffic stop on {STREET} for an equipment violation, officers recovered an unlicensed handgun from the vehicle; the {AGE}-year-old subject had no FOID or concealed-carry license and was arrested and charged with unlawful use of a weapon. IUCR 1430 (unlawful use/possession of weapon), reported under NIBRS as a weapons-law violation. The firearm was inventoried as evidence and run through LEADS for any stolen status. Disposition: {DISPOSITION}. No shots fired, no injuries. Including this in the monthly weapons-offense tally for the board.

Mayor Okonkwo, for your awareness — report {CASE} was filed {DATE} at {TIME} for criminal trespass at {ADDRESS}. A {AGE}-year-old subject was found inside a fenced, posted commercial property after hours; the subject had been warned previously and was taken into custody. IUCR 2870 (criminal trespass to real property), reported under NIBRS as trespass of real property. No property was taken and no damage was found. Disposition: {DISPOSITION}. The property owner was notified and a trespass complaint signed. Routine — keeping it in the daily blotter.

Mayor, good-news heads-up on incident {CASE}. On {DATE} at {TIME}, during a patrol check on {STREET}, an officer located a vehicle previously reported stolen out of a neighboring jurisdiction; the vehicle was recovered undamaged and valued at approximately {AMOUNT}. The LEADS hit confirmed the theft status, forensics were requested, and the registered owner was notified. IUCR 0910 cleared by recovery; reported under NIBRS as recovered stolen property. Disposition: {DISPOSITION}. No occupants at recovery. I'll note the recovery in the property/evidence log and the weekly summary.
reply_tmpl: Nice recovery — pass along my thanks to the patrol officer. This is worth a line in the next newsletter.

body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, for your awareness — report {CASE} was filed {DATE} at {TIME} for a theft from a porch at {ADDRESS}. The complainant reported a delivered package taken; carrier tracking confirmed delivery and the loss is estimated at {AMOUNT}. IUCR 0890 (theft from building / porch piracy), reported under NIBRS as larceny. No camera footage was available at this address, but officers canvassed nearby {STREET} cameras. Disposition: {DISPOSITION}. We've had several of these this month — I'll bundle them in the weekly index-crime detail and records can support a porch-theft community alert if you'd like.
reply_tmpl: Let's do the porch-theft alert ahead of the holidays. Send me a draft to review.

body_tmpl: Mayor, flagging incident {CASE} filed {DATE} at {TIME} for a burglary from a motor vehicle at {ADDRESS}. The complainant reported a passenger window smashed overnight and a bag plus electronics removed, estimated loss {AMOUNT}. IUCR 0915 / 0610 as applicable (theft from motor vehicle), reported under NIBRS. No suspect information; officers recovered glass and processed for prints. Disposition: {DISPOSITION}. This is one of three vehicle entries on that block this week — I've asked for extra overnight passes on {STREET} and will track the cluster in the weekly trend report.
